mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate decline exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals improving labor market absorption and rising consumer spending, generating positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE > 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital goods demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction and rising input costs, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not predict negative 5-day returns during tightening periods, or the signal reverses direction when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 0.3% month-over-month triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above threshold is followed by positive 20-day returns in >55% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment indicates robust job creation and labor market tightening, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect is dominated by single-series noise.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2.0% is followed by negative 60-day returns in >50% of quarters, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 90.0 index points signals severe manufacturing contraction and weak business investment, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO below 90.0 is followed by positive 5-day returns in >65% of cases, or the threshold loses predictive significance after controlling for monetary policy.
